scope.time_window: 2025-12-18 ± 7 days, plus any record within 7-day transitive closure of R000465

policy_questions.description: Do R000465 and adjacent same-employee same-type records within 7 days trigger Article 11 split-circumvention check?
Does each individual record comply with training fee standard (3500/person/class)?
Are there any reused invoices or duplicate claims?
Historical comparison: would 2022 deprecated policy yield a different conclusion for the same records?

data_questions: All training_fee records for E0041 (list_expenses, done)
Full detail for each record in the 7-day cluster (get_expense_detail, done)
Invoice usage info for each invoice in the cluster (find_invoice_usage)
evidence_requirements: Policy text for Attachment 2 approval tiers
Record details with dates and amounts

reasons: One primary rule (Article 11 split-circumvention) with batch grouping and 7-day date boundary
Multiple supporting policies (authorization tiers, training standard)
Aggregation of 4 records within 7-day sliding window

policy_researcher.task: Compare 2022 deprecated policy and 2025 current policy for the split-circumvention clause and approval thresholds. Determine if the same record cluster (E0041, training_fee, Dec 18-25 2025, total 9764.73) would trigger different conclusions under each version.

independent_reviewer.task: Review the 7-day boundary, AR tier threshold application, and whether the records truly constitute 'split to circumvent' or are legitimate separate training events.

stopping_conditions: All 4+ cluster records verified for invoice uniqueness
